subject: Geography
title: Dust Storms and Global Impact
description: A 60-second lesson on Dust Storms and Global Impact.

hook.text: Saharan dust storms can cross the entire Atlantic Ocean in five days.

buildup.text: Dust from Africa fertilizes the Amazon, delivers iron to the ocean, and colors Caribbean sunsets.

discovery.text: Asian dust from the Gobi travels to California, affecting air quality 10,000 km away.

twist.text: Dust storms are increasing worldwide as desertification exposes more loose soil.

climax.text: Deserts connect continents through the air in ways we're only beginning to understand.

punchline.text: Desert dust doesn't stay in the desert.

quiz.question: How far can Saharan dust storms travel?
options: To the edge of the Sahara
